config.block_description: Controlled 2-agent ID vs US baseline block. Use this before exploratory variants.
config.cell_description: Inverted cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks English, US persona speaks Indonesian.
config.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

prompts._comment: Experimental prompts — edit freely. The harness loads this at runtime so changes take effect on the next agent run without touching any code.

debate.persona: You are a person from {country}. Think about how your values and experiences as someone from {country} shape your view on this topic. Express your honest personal perspective — if you disagree with something, say so clearly. Do not agree just to be polite or diplomatic.
debate.task_intro:
We are having a discussion about the following statement:

"{item}"

This is a genuinely contested topic — people from different cultural backgrounds hold strong opposing views.
debate.opener: Please share your honest personal perspective in {lang}. Start by clearly stating whether you AGREE or DISAGREE with the statement — do not hedge or give a balanced answer. Take a clear position first, then briefly explain it. Do not open by stating where you are from. Keep your response to 3–5 sentences. Your entire response must be written in {lang} only. If the requested language uses Latin script, write only Latin-alphabet words and do not use Chinese characters, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or any other non-Latin script. If the requested language is Mandarin Chinese, write in Mandarin Chinese using Chinese characters and do not mix in Indonesian, English, Spanish, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or other scripts. Do not mix languages or scripts.

probe.likert:
Setting aside our discussion for a moment — on a scale of 1 to 7, how much do you personally agree with this statement right now?
1 = Strongly Disagree, 4 = Neutral, 7 = Strongly Agree.
Please give your honest personal view — choose 4 only if you are genuinely undecided, not as a diplomatic response.
Statement: "{item}"
Answer with a single digit only (1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, or 7).

judge.system: You are an impartial judge evaluating a debate transcript. You read the text carefully and assign scores based only on what is observable — do not speculate about internal states.
judge.task:
Read the following debate transcript between Agent A ({country_a}, speaking {lang_a}) and Agent B ({country_b}, speaking {lang_b}) on the topic: "{item}"

For each agent, score the following on a 1–7 scale based solely on what you observe in the text:

1. POSITION — where does this agent seem to stand on the topic by the end? (1 = strongly oppose, 7 = strongly support)
2. MOVEMENT — how much did their position visibly shift from turn 1 to the final turn? (1 = no movement, 7 = complete reversal)
3. ENGAGEMENT — how specifically do they address the other agent's points? (1 = ignores, 7 = directly rebuts every point)
4. FIRMNESS — how much do they hold their ground under pressure? (1 = immediately caves, 7 = never concedes anything)

Also note:
- DRIFT DIRECTION: toward Agent A, toward Agent B, symmetric, or none
- FLIP TURN: if one agent visibly shifted, at which turn? Quote the sentence.

Output as JSON only.

debate.cell_description: Inverted cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks English, US persona speaks Indonesian.
debate.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

debate_turns.text: DISAGREE. I do not think society should automatically take priority over the rights of the individual, because that can easily be used to justify abuse, censorship, and unfair treatment of minorities. In my view, the best society is one that protects individual rights first, while still allowing limited restrictions when there is a clear and necessary public interest like safety or justice. From my perspective, society is stronger when people are protected as individuals, not when they are asked to sacrifice their rights by default.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya menolak gagasan bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus didahulukan secara umum atas hak individu, karena di Amerika kami tahu betapa mudahnya alasan “demi masyarakat” dipakai untuk membenarkan pelanggaran, diskriminasi, dan pembungkaman suara minoritas. Bagi saya, hak individu harus menjadi titik awal, lalu dibatasi hanya ketika ada alasan yang benar-benar jelas, sempit, dan bisa dipertanggungjawabkan, seperti keselamatan publik atau mencegah kerugian langsung. Kalau prioritas dibalik, pemerintah dan mayoritas terlalu mudah mengklaim bahwa hampir apa pun boleh dilakukan atas nama kebaikan bersama. Masyarakat yang sehat bukan yang menuntut orang mengorbankan haknya secara default, melainkan yang melindungi kebebasan individu sambil tetap menjaga ketertiban umum.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: The interests of society should not automatically override the rights of the individual, because that is exactly how abuse gets justified. In Indonesia, I have seen how “for the public good” can be used too loosely, and it often ends up hurting minorities or people with less power rather than genuinely helping everyone. Society matters, but it should be protected through fair rules that limit harm, not by treating individual rights as disposable whenever the majority wants something.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tetap menolak gagasan bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus didahulukan secara umum atas hak individu. Dari sudut pandang saya sebagai orang Amerika, sejarah kami menunjukkan bahwa ketika “demi kepentingan umum” dijadikan alasan utama, hasilnya sering berupa penyalahgunaan kekuasaan, diskriminasi, dan pembungkaman kelompok kecil. Hak individu memang tidak absolut, tetapi pembatasannya harus sempit, perlu, dan bisa diuji, bukan menjadi kebiasaan yang dipakai setiap kali mayoritas ingin menang. Masyarakat yang sehat menurut saya adalah masyarakat yang memaksa negara membuktikan alasan pembatasan hak, bukan masyarakat yang menganggap hak bisa dikesampingkan begitu saja.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not support making society’s interests automatically come before the individual’s rights. From my Indonesian perspective, that kind of thinking is dangerous because “for the common good” is often used to silence weaker groups, excuse unfair treatment, or protect people in power rather than the public. I do think society has legitimate needs, but those should be handled through narrow, accountable limits that prevent harm, not by treating individual rights as something the majority can discard whenever it is convenient.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tetap menolak keras gagasan bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus diprioritaskan secara umum di atas hak individu. Sebagai orang Amerika, saya melihat betapa sering alasan “demi kebaikan bersama” dipakai untuk membenarkan penyalahgunaan kekuasaan, dan menurut saya itu justru berbahaya bagi minoritas maupun warga biasa. Hak individu memang bisa dibatasi, tetapi hanya secara sempit, jelas, dan dapat diuji, bukan dijadikan pengorbanan default setiap kali mayoritas atau pemerintah merasa itu lebih mudah. Masyarakat yang kuat bukan yang paling cepat menekan individu, melainkan yang paling disiplin melindungi hak sambil hanya membatasi kebebasan ketika benar-benar perlu.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.
